text:
Real market alpha isn’t loud confidence; it’s the capacity to stay in contact with fear long enough to read the signal instead of flinching from it. 

The market punishes comfort. The founders and investors who separate themselves are the ones who can distinguish “this feels dangerous because it’s stupid” from “this feels dangerous because it will rewire my map.”

text:
one of the many great examples of how something seemingly frivolous (an industry focused on “make car go fast”) led to lives being saved

once you really internalize the implications of this, you never dismiss anything as frivolous ever

quoted_tweet.text:
The best F1 partnership ever was when the Ferrari pit crew team helped a UK Children's Hospital improve its ICU hand-off process in the 1990s.

A new protocol dropped the error rate from 30% to 10%.

How? After reviewing surgery operation footage, Ferrari F1 advised:

▫️a new delivery theatre layout
▫️new checklists and to only give 1-2 tasks per person
▫️to assign a “lollipop man” (this is the pit crew person that holds a sign on a long stick and only waves a driver through after making sure everyone else on the team has put the tires on)

text:
Btw when I say 9-5 lifestyle this doesn't mean I'm championing being employed over doing your own thing. All I'm saying is that if you ever end up breaking out of the traditional system, which is more mental than it is physical, you'll probably come to realize that a lot of your frustrations were misguided

Whether you work with others or it's just you, have steady income or eat what you kill, my personal view is that this idea of doing whatever you want, whenever you want, is an idea that only sounds intoxicating when you've never been able to experience it before

Waking up and going to bed at random times every day, doing things when you "feel like it," quickly turns into feeling like you want to do nothing. Worst I've felt was when I'd finish my morning coffee and have to think about what to do next, and these were periods where finances were the least of my worries too

Contrasted with the best I've felt, which always involved feeling like I didn't have time to waste. Brain is ready to be used in the morning, put on an outfit that says "I want the world to notice I'm here," step out of my house to invoke the senses, post up somewhere that gets me in the zone, and make the most of this precious energy

Energy shifts a few hours later, unwind a bit by interacting with people, sharing laughs, reminding yourself that you're still very much human and an active part of the flow of society. Tie up some loose ends, come back home to mark the transition into a more restful mode, head hits the pillow knowing you provided various types of value to the world

Intentional, unforced structure that respects the body's desire to move to a rhythm. A routine you get to lock into, as opposed to have to endure. Doesn't look too different from the "old life," but feels completely different now

quoted_tweet.text:
The 9-5 lifestyle was the real alpha all along. Start the morning by getting out of the house. Lock into a few hours of deep focus. Socialize with fellow humans when you want a break. Retreat back to your sanctuary after a long day of progress

The only reason it feels like a prison is because you're being forced to do it. But have your own autonomy and make your own rules, many of you will eventually come to structure your routine this way again by choice after experiencing the insidious comfort of undisciplined freedom

text: Exhaustion that comes from inaction is infinitely worse than exhaustion that comes from action. Feel like you have too much on your plate, the alternative is literally letting thoughts fester in your mind until you go insane. Store that reminder in the back pocket. Use it often

text:
The discrepancy between the internet and real life is stark. Spend a day online and it feels like the world is plummeting to zero. Then you go outside and see multiple stores with a line out the door

Creates immense cognitive dissonance. How can everyone afford all of this stuff? How can they be so happy when it's the worst time in history to be alive? Alternatively, how are there so many people on the feed who are seemingly on the verge of ruin? Do they not realize that life isn't as bad as they think?  

Too blissful, you feel like you're being psyopped and it's only a matter of time before you get left behind. Too cognizant of the future, you also feel like you're being psyopped and you just need to touch grass again

This perpetual state of having to talk yourself into and out of various stances, borderline mental illness. Hard to not go crazy. Validity to be acknowledged in both. But it's on you to figure out the right mix of what to believe, and it's something we're all trying to figure out together

text:
I thought that sentence was pretty self explanatory but what I mean is before i got the corp job i thought it would be gay & i’d hate it but it was actually fun af, got paid 6-figs for a job i did on literal autopilot & got off at 2:30pm, had all the homies there, and was “the guy”

Experiencing that made me realize exactly why people never leave. It hits every paychological human need at the exact same time lmao. U have to be lowkey sick in the head & actually about ur growth to bail on that. If i didn’t have the desire to turn my yearly income into my monthly income i would have never bailed. But soul overtook comfort & i needed aventure+risk+fun. I knew my potential was way higher than the world’s smoothest/safest 6 figures

A lot of life actually works this way. You think you’ll hate something (and will probably tell people how gay it is) BEFORE you even actually experience it yourself. then when you ACTUALLY experience it, You’re like “oh I get it”

Once i went through this experience I stopped making judgements on things I had never directly experienced because I was very aware how much of an asshat that made me

“Corp jobs are gay bro I could never get a corp job!” - guy that’s never had a corp job

quoted_tweet.text: @retirementkeys “Before i got that job i thought it would be gay then when i left i 100% understood why most never do” what do you mean by this?

thread:
I thought that sentence was pretty self explanatory but what I mean is before i got the corp job i thought it would be gay & i’d hate it but it was actually fun af, got paid 6-figs for a job i did on literal autopilot & got off at 2:30pm, had all the homies there, and was “the guy”

Experiencing that made me realize exactly why people never leave. It hits every paychological human need at the exact same time lmao. U have to be lowkey sick in the head & actually about ur growth to bail on that. If i didn’t have the desire to turn my yearly income into my monthly income i would have never bailed. But soul overtook comfort & i needed aventure+risk+fun. I knew my potential was way higher than the world’s smoothest/safest 6 figures

A lot of life actually works this way. You think you’ll hate something (and will probably tell people how gay it is) BEFORE you even actually experience it yourself. then when you ACTUALLY experience it, You’re like “oh I get it”

Once i went through this experience I stopped making judgements on things I had never directly experienced because I was very aware how much of an asshat that made me

“Corp jobs are gay bro I could never get a corp job!” - guy that’s never had a corp job

I will also say there was monstrous identity pain for me being associated with literal incapable emotionally-stunted normietards

I didn’t give a fuck about the fact iw as getting paid more / was “better”, me doing the SAME THING as some retarded bitch doing drugs in the bathroom while casting spells on me in her free time made a part of me hate myself. It’s like “what the fuck is you doing buddy” type shit lmfao. If u settle for bottomfeeder-accessible shit you’ll have always a little bottomfeeder alive inside of you

Some dude will shart himself while drinking a big gulp and you’ll be like “the fact we’re both in the same place is fucking absolutely astronomical”. And you’re actually right lol. U playing way 2 small but avoiding the work required to shoot upwards.

Get on that magic carpet kang. Spread dem wings @ fly 🆙🆙🆙

You also get psychefucked by the hiveprogramming. Unless ur psyche is strong enough to snap spells you’ll probably get fucked mentally

I had failed a company & jumped in with ambitious & take-over-world energy. I literally asked sales manager in my interview “if one car is going 120mpg while all others are going 35pmh will the 120mph car get paid more”. I knew i was the best & it would just be bloodshet on scoreboard. I just needed cash for my next diceroll

But when u actually enter into it it’s infinite social proof/assumption that you’re literally retarded. You are IN the cage of the babies, and it is endless subconscious programming that tells you “hey you’re retarded!”. But it’s not for YOU specifically, it’s because you’re with people that LITERALLY need it.

The guy sharting himself from his 9th big gulp & the single mom with 3 baby daddys & a drug problem actually do need it at a 1st grade level. That is why i got offended at first. And then when i realized the truth i was like “woah”

You really do have to hate yourself to put up with that in a way - with rare exceptions

text:
If you post anything genuinely truthful/helpful you’ll quickly realize how monstrous the % of people are that are ABSOLUTELY fucking cooked IS, based on their own voluntary willingness to stay cooked. Totally self-prescribed, entirely willingly

“Nobody understands me or believes in me!”. No shit. They are literally all nondiscerning retards afraid of literally everything unimportant (including you rising). Just tune it out.

Basically nobody “gets it”. If someone does get it - that is SO fucking rare it’s genuinely astronomical when you find someone that does. Ur on some mad max type shit. “Oh, you have a soul? Hi”

Don’t hinge your psychological or spiritual health on “does the group accept me”. If THAT is your aim, you are DOOMED to be a perpetually caged, uninteresting loser who just copes & replyguys through life as he pokes people from the anon shadows

Instead hinge ur psyche on “did i courageously fade the bullshit around me to pursue what I was pulled to pursue on my life & generate my actions based on the map God placed into MY soul. Did i give MYSELF permission to go MY WAY..”

Hinge it on if your ACTIONS are aligned with your own SELF. If the ACTIONS are in agreement with your soul

If THAT answer is yes, you WILL be okay. 

You’ll probably have a lot of mouthbreathers mad/coping at you & firing off little soyremarks 24/7 as they attack ur sweg, but who gives a fuck

“Is the path i’m walking aligning with my soul or not”.

And it takes $0 to start there (shifting the foundational alignment/path your life is going). And even if you “don’t know what to do”, you KNOW where to look & where to start. You KNOW what to “pivot” your life towarda to “make it feel more nourishing/fun/enjoyable”. What sounds fucking fun to you? Go do that. 

Out of MIND, into HEART

text:
When you truly want it, you’ll get it.

Ur kinda worried now that u might not get it but its more of an intellectual worry. Not a soul worry. U haven’t TRULY decided cuz u don’t TRULY want it yet.

The moment you truly decide, it will come quicker & more effortlessly than you could ever imagine.

But a large part of the game is trying to figure out why you can’t get yourself to decide
thread:
When you truly want it, you’ll get it.

Ur kinda worried now that u might not get it but its more of an intellectual worry. Not a soul worry. U haven’t TRULY decided cuz u don’t TRULY want it yet.

The moment you truly decide, it will come quicker & more effortlessly than you could ever imagine.

But a large part of the game is trying to figure out why you can’t get yourself to decide

There’s a soft confidence that comes with the knowing that “when you decide it will be so” but that confidence is quickly overshadowed by the uncertainty of not knowing exactly when you’ll make that full decision, so you’re quite literally in exact same spot

God/nature plays more of a part of things than we’d like to believe. U can evolve out of things but who controls the speed of that evolution? It’s not you. All you can do is listen & direct your attention & keep continually steering the spiritual momentum ship in ur favor.

“Divine timing” in the sense that the divine is what controls the timing, because weirdly enough the divine is what controls the making of the decision. Everyone wants to full decide, but can’t. So *they*, literally, don’t have control. And this applies even after u’ve separated other’s wants from ur own wants. Can really only ride the wave & press the gas as best u can until that final domino somehow falls that gives u that extra wind in the sail to get u there. But it was never acitivty/tasks that pushed over edge, it was internal stance it all came from

“You’ll meet your fate on that path you took to avoid it”

text:
The wildest part about the corp world for me was how the adults are literally children

When I first got that job I was actually slightly offended because I thought the manager was being a patronizing asshole

Then i realized later on "oh no he's just genuinely trying to be understood by literal retards"

Then also realized how u can just gigafarm aura because if you have even the slightest modicum of competence ur basically a goldenchild. It's like if u were upper-echelon AAU player & then u decided to play w/ ur town team. It's like charity. Except when u do it in corp the soys rally against u & the women try to impose themselves as ur work wives but u have 2 fade all poon because u don't shit where u eat & ur a man of purity

I knew day 1 my stay was temporary.
thread:
The wildest part about the corp world for me was how the adults are literally children

When I first got that job I was actually slightly offended because I thought the manager was being a patronizing asshole

Then i realized later on "oh no he's just genuinely trying to be understood by literal retards"

Then also realized how u can just gigafarm aura because if you have even the slightest modicum of competence ur basically a goldenchild. It's like if u were upper-echelon AAU player & then u decided to play w/ ur town team. It's like charity. Except when u do it in corp the soys rally against u & the women try to impose themselves as ur work wives but u have 2 fade all poon because u don't shit where u eat & ur a man of purity

I knew day 1 my stay was temporary.

One time I was leaving office & this chick & i enter doorway at same time & she literally goes "oh you go first, you're way more attractive than me"

I was like bitch, what?

i wish I was joking. U can literally aurafarm ur way to sexual harassment in corp EASILY.

Ngl I lowkey loved the dumb office politics & aurafarming on normies. Also had fantastic friends there that i am still best friends with that i still talk to basically daily

One time a 41 yr old coworker asked me to get a coffee with him so i said fuck it & he tells me in a starbucks he summons demons & can use them whenever I need them if i need them

I said i didn’t need to sickem on any1 but asked how he got into it & he told me. He then brings up how he’s super skeptical of this jacked dude with a mustache and if i’ve ever seen him doing anything shady

It’s like in corp there’s all these characters from a movie. Like somehow these people are just doing life going about their shit with their quarrels & vendettas & whatever else they are into

Wild to think about. Corp very interesting lol. Is fun tho.

I think one of the reasons i enjoyed it was because i never identified with it. I viewed it as some dumb experiment that i figured i’d just become the best at while i was in it. Had failed a company & needed $$$ so knew i was jumping once i reloaded my chips. I just viewed it like a playground for social war strategy & social dynamics so it was mostly joy. Also had a boss that i learned a LOT about leadership from. Dude held down insane company culture. Had that shit on lock. Then one day I literally told him that - i said “brother without you the culture would literally be nonexistent lmao” and he goes “…who told you i’m leaving?” & i go “you just did” & then that ignited a wild fucking next chapter 

If ur corp job has good culture it is 100% golden handcuffs. Before i got that job i thought it would be gay then when i left i 100% understood why most never do

thread:
if ur broke and hate ur life just make $2-3k/mo on the internet, sell ur shit, cut ur lease and move somewhere cheap & sunny

U will find joy once again

When i was in corprison i just wanted to hit 2k/mo on the interwebs. I was like "man if i could just get to 2k a fucking month i'd just chill on the beach and life would be fucking amazing"

And i was actually wrong. It wasn't amazing, it was giga-amazing. Like 500x better than I imagined.

Fully worth it in every way. Once u hit that & free urself at babylevel its like fuckin magical rainbows & infinite bliss. It's like galactic euphoria being perma-dripped into your veins & thru ur psyche. Ur laying in the sun w/ cash coming in & nowhere to be/go. HOLY FUCK. WAY BETTER than you EVEN IMAGINE.

U wake up whenever, get some sun, get a mango smoothie & bring ur laptop with u, pretend to work, eat some delicious chicken breast by the beach with another smoothie, send 3 emails, say "wow that was tough, break time". Put ur laptop away (you did nothing), tan on beach w/ another mango smoothie, go home & shower, lift, eat delicious dinner, send 2 more emails, accidentally pull 9some cuz ur aura is so strong, shower again, then just sit under the stars in the warm evening night just smiling at the sky

text:
Very common normie sticking point is that in life you'll be in a situation where something that served you at one point in your life no longer serves you

You probably spent energy, effort, time etc designing that thing & getting it exactly how you wanted it for what you wanted back THEN

But inevitably things change & you end up in a "new internal situation" with an identical external situation except now instead of the work being "worth it" you're just existing within a self-made prison. 3 "hurdles:

1 - accepting/acknowledging the truth that you've evolved internally & what used to serve you no longer does
2 - accepting the required "swallowing of the sunk cost" that needs to take place once 1 is accepted
3 - having the difficult convos required to tear away the thing from anyone who's become attached to it

3-headed wall u gotta pierce thru, but once you do it the first time u can more easily recognize it / get through them

Always tough tho. Easier to recognize that you've evolved internally, but the sunk cost is ALWAYS hard to swallow and the convos are ALWAYS tough to have
thread:
Very common normie sticking point is that in life you'll be in a situation where something that served you at one point in your life no longer serves you

You probably spent energy, effort, time etc designing that thing & getting it exactly how you wanted it for what you wanted back THEN

But inevitably things change & you end up in a "new internal situation" with an identical external situation except now instead of the work being "worth it" you're just existing within a self-made prison. 3 "hurdles:

1 - accepting/acknowledging the truth that you've evolved internally & what used to serve you no longer does
2 - accepting the required "swallowing of the sunk cost" that needs to take place once 1 is accepted
3 - having the difficult convos required to tear away the thing from anyone who's become attached to it

3-headed wall u gotta pierce thru, but once you do it the first time u can more easily recognize it / get through them

Always tough tho. Easier to recognize that you've evolved internally, but the sunk cost is ALWAYS hard to swallow and the convos are ALWAYS tough to have

An easy start to transitioning out of this situation & into the one you *truly* want is asking yourself "what do I know is true here?" & plotting those data points from all sides. Just "seeing" everything that's been stuck as an unarticulated blob, helps

& not sure if this helps btw but every single time I've ever been in this situation (and it has been a fuckton), I ended up exploding the bullshit I hated & felt 1,000,000% better on the other fucking side, every single time - no matter how "dumb" leaving that shit behind was "on paper"

The soul always knows. If ur over it, fuck it. Don't let opinions stop u from leading ur life. If u boil it down, that's really all ur up against. A fleeting comment

I also have always started with "what do I fucking hate about this". I call them "thorn in sides". Touched on it in the Godenergy vid on my yt

I first identify what I hate and ask myself something like "can I/ is there a way to design this out?" or "is this even necessary?" or "within the things I hate, is there a way to easily solve the most painful part of it?"

Sometimes the answer is yes & u can make it better for urself. othertimes ur just in the wrong shit

text:
1 right move in the right direction that takes you 5 minutes

Gets you farther than 5,000 wrong moves in the wrong direction

Activity does NOT equal progress. 

The internal desire to "work hard" to prove to yourself that you "work hard" will fuck you more than it helps you

text:
You can make more money than you ever have in a year by:

- Getting something to sell
- running traffic to it

If you drop ur perfectionism & stop being gay & literally JUST DO THAT (other people have products and driving traffic is not hard) YOU WILL MAKE MONEY EVENTUALLY.

People think making a dollar is like some magical fucking sacred impossibletask. Like bro just turn brain off “get muh thing to sell run muh traffic”

Lmao. U got it. Just go QUICK

The only thing that keeps anything in check is the lowest rung on your internal “personal security” ladder

Only when something falls down to a level of making you physically uncomfortable will you actually do anything. And this “lowest rung” RAISING is what moves ball fwd on most shit at high speeds. This applies to work in the micro as well. U can stare at screen for 20 mins and poke around until the unacceptability of your own work standard kicks in & u get pissed, grab machete & finally start thrashing. “Productivity” is largely ur ability to shorten the “poke around” window in the micro & train a more savage mindset. It’s RARELY EVER a “focus” issue, it’s “being gay” issue, no matter how tiny (or large) that window may be. It is hunter mode, not “feed me” mode

In the macro - this applies to the position ur life is currently in. Problem though for most is that rung of “i’m in danger” is stuck UNDERNEATH where they tend to maintain their life. Even though their life “sucks”, its still not sucky enough to trigger the safety mechanism & fuel them to do anything about it

Eventually you gain control over your psyche and can get yourself to do stuff but hurdle at ground 0 is ironically the toughest because you have nothing to lose, so you can’t really go down. If u feel safe in squalor (from either a root chakra OR odentity POV) you’ll live & exist forever in squalor
